author: Matthew Ford
description: Wayfinder Maps turns a GitHub repo's issues into a live picture of what to work on next. Each issue labeled wayfinder:map renders as a head card with its tickets laid out in a dependency-layered tree, - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: ocuclaw
manifest.description: Visualizes wayfinder maps (from Matt Pocock's skills workflow) as dependency-layered ticket trees from GitHub issues, with frontier highlighting and one-click /wayfinder command copying.
manifest.name: Wayfinder Maps

name: Wayfinder Maps

releases.author: ocuclaw
releases.description:
## What's new

- Add an **All maps / focused map picker** to quickly isolate one map.
- Add persistent controls to **show or hide completed maps** and **show only incomplete tickets**.
- Add a responsive **Hybrid** view with dependency tree and actionability list side by side on wide panes and stacked on narrow panes.
- Remember map focus, display filters, view mode, and zoom per device.
- Keep global tallies stable across map focus and display filters.

## Quality

- Add pure projection and actionability-grouping tests.
- Preserve fail-closed handling for unverifiable blockers.
- Support Obsidian 1.7.2 and later, including the imperative settings fallback on versions before 1.13.

All unit, build, live smoke, and directory-lint gates passed for this release.

releases.author: ocuclaw
releases.description: Restores the settings tab on Obsidian versions older than 1.13: the declarative settings tab (getSettingDefinitions) from 1.0.1 now has the documented display() fallback for pre-1.13 apps, and minAppVersion drops to 1.7.2 (the real floor, set by async revealLeaf). All submission-review fixes from 1.0.1 are included.

releases.author: ocuclaw
releases.description: Community-plugin submission review fixes: declarative settings tab (getSettingDefinitions, Obsidian 1.13), awaited revealLeaf, createFragment, window-scoped requestAnimationFrame, setCssStyles for dynamic styles, typed ToolbarControls callbacks. minAppVersion is now 1.13.0.

releases.author: ocuclaw
releases.description:
Wayfinder for Obsidian 1.0.0 — the first stable release.

Renders [wayfinder](https://github.com/mattpocock/skills) maps from GitHub issues as dependency-layered ticket trees inside Obsidian: frontier highlighting, delegation tallies, live claim checks, and one-click `/wayfinder` command copying. Desktop and mobile. Plugin 1.0.0 is aligned to the v1.1.0 skills release.

## Highlights of the road from 0.1 to 1.0

- **0.1–0.2 — the view**: dependency-layered trees per `wayfinder:map`, ticket detail modal with live comments, list mode (mobile default), zoom (toolbar / ctrl+wheel / pinch), collapsible maps, focus-sync, change flashes, and the "Copy /wayfinder for the next takeable ticket" command.
- **0.3 — correctness**: fail-closed frontier (unverified blockers never glow), a universal pre-action claim guard, next-takeable fixes.
- **0.4 — robustness**: error taxonomy for GitHub failures, pagination, sync coalescing, repo-scoped snapshot caching.
- **0.5 — polish**: light theme, compact mobile toolbar, accessibility, scroll preservation across re-renders.
- **0.6 — workflow**: delegation queue with batch handoffs (selection mode), bottleneck (`unblocks N`) and claimed-idle staleness indicators, need-you / agent-ready / either tallies.
- **0.7 — code health**: deterministic offline tests, modular view.
- **0.8 — pre-1.0 hardening** (13 fixes): blockers carry state + repo identity and fail closed when invisible; failed dependency fetches mark data unverified and warn; the claim guard fails closed with an explicit "Copy anyway" override; batch copies preflight every ticket; conflicting labels resolve conservatively to HITL; "Part of #N" only matches a real metadata line; secondary rate limits are reported as rate limits; test connection actually probes Issues access; ARIA-valid cards; clean unload.
- **0.9 — multi-repo**: configure any number of repos, each with its own fine-grained token; parallel sync with per-repo errors; a repo filter in the toolbar; automatic migration of single-repo settings.
- **1.0.0**: final review pass over the 0.8–0.9 work (guard edge cases, filter-state purity, UI ordering).

## Install

Copy `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/wayfinder/`, enable **Wayfinder** in Settings → Community plugins, then add a repo + fine-grained PAT (read-only Issues) in Settings → Wayfinder.

Verified against the live pipeline: 35/35 unit tests, 9/9 smoke checks.
